rulings.Case Title: TYLON J. HUNT VS. CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Erroneously Entered Default
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 6, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Set Aside Erroneously Entered Default. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to set aside the default entered against it on September 25, 2017 is granted. Based on a preponderance of the evidence standard, the court finds that the City was not properly served with the summons and complaint and thus the City's motion is not limited to the time frame set forth in CCP 473(b). Granting the motion comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits and plaintiff Tylon Hunt has not identified any legal prejudice he would suffer by the granting of the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the City is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MELODY ESQUIVEL ET AL VS. SIMONMED IMAGING, PC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Plaintiffs Individual Paga Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 6, 2018, Line 6, PLAINTIFFS MELODY ESQUIVEL, MARGARET CLEE, AND ABNINDER BAINS' Motion To Approve Plaintiffs' Individual PAGA Settlement. Plaintiffs' motion to approve plaintiffs' individual PAGA settlement is denied without prejudice to refiling the motion based on a fuller and more persuasive record. No proof of service of the moving papers on the LWDA. The settlement agreement is not included in the moving papers. There is no persuasive explanation why plaintiffs sought to represent other allegedly aggrieved employees and then agreed to a settlement providing them no relief. Most significantly, there is no qualitative or quantitative analysis for the plaintiffs' PAGA claims nor is there any persuasive explanation why so little of the settlement amount has been allocated to PAGA penalties. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BENNETT GOLDBERG ET AL VS. STEPHENS INSTITUTE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 6, 2018, Line 9, PLAINTIFFS BENNETT GOLDBERG, LINDA KUCKUK'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents. Judge Kahn, who is hearing this motion, discloses that he served as the settlement judge in the "Government Enforcement case" brought by the City, but he does not believe that disqualifies him from deciding motions in this case. Plaintiffs Bennett Goldberg and Linda Kuckuk's motion to compel further responses to requests for production of documents is granted in part. Defendant Stephens Institute is required to: a) provide further responses and produce all documents responsive to requests 2 and 3; b) provide further responses and produce documents responsive to requests 1, 6, 12 that relate to and cover the time period May 13, 2015 through March 30, 2017; and c) at its option, either respond to special interrogatory 1 as set forth in the order on plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories or provide further responses and produce documents responsive to request 13 limited to the most recent document concerning each person who signed a bed license between May 13, 2015 and March 30, 2017 that contains contact information for that person and all non-contact information about that person may be redacted. The Academy is not required to provide further responses or produce any documents as to requests 4 and 5. The portions of this motion that are denied are being denied without prejudice to plaintiffs requesting the documents requested or some subset thereof at a later date on a fuller showing of discovery relevance than has been made in the moving papers. Plaintiffs have not shown that there is any arguable viability to their delayed discovery allegations and their requests for many documents outside the time period May 13, 2015 through March 30, 2017 without limitation as to time are overbroad and lacking in discovery relevance. All documents regarding the LLCs' acquisition and transfer of interests in the buildings that are the subject of this case meet the standard of discovery relevance. Plaintiffs have not shown that the identities of the owners of the LLCs or the formations of the LLCs meet the standard of discovery relevance. Request 13 has been limited to require only contact information of the putative class and such information needs to be provided without need for a notice to any of the putative class members. Further responses are due no later than August 6, 2017. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Academy is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BENNETT GOLDBERG ET AL VS. STEPHENS INSTITUTE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 6, 2018, Line 9, PLAINTIFFS BENNETT GOLDBERG, LINDA KUCKUK'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories. Judge Kahn, who is hearing this motion, discloses that he served as the settlement judge in the "Government Enforcement" case brought by the City, but he does not believe that disqualifies him from deciding motions in this case. Plaintiffs Bennett Goldberg and Linda Kuckuk's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories is granted in part. Defendant Stephens Institute is required to: a) provide further responses to interrogatories 2 and 3 that relate to and cover the time period May 13, 2015 through March 30, 2017; and b) at its option, either produce the documents required to be produced in response to request 13 as set forth in the order on plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to requests for production of documents or provide further responses to interrogatory 1 limited to the most recent contact information for each person who signed a bed license between May 13, 2015 and March 30, 2017. The Academy is not required to provide further responses to interrogatories 6 and 7. The portions of this motion that are denied are being denied without prejudice to plaintiffs requesting the documents requested or some subset thereof at a later date on a fuller showing of discovery relevance than has been made in the moving papers. Plaintiffs have not shown that there is any arguable viability to their delayed discovery allegations and their requests for considerable amount of information outside the time period May 13, 2015 through March 30, 2017 without limitation as to time are overbroad and lacking in discovery relevance. Plaintiffs have not shown that the identities of the owners of the LLCs meet the standard of discovery relevance. Responsive information to interrogatory 1 needs to be provided without need for a notice to any of the putative class members. Further responses are due no later than August 6, 2017. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Academy is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BENNETT GOLDBERG ET AL VS. STEPHENS INSTITUTE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories 12.1 To 12.7
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 6, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT STEPHENS INSTITUTE'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories 12.1 To 12.7. Judge Kahn, who is hearing this motion, discloses that he served as the settlement judge in the "Government Enforcement" case brought by the City, but he does not believe that disqualifies him from deciding motions in this case. Defendant Stephens Institute's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories 12.1 through 12.7 is granted in its entirety. Plaintiffs Bennett Goldberg and Linda Kuckuk are required to provide further responses to each of these interrogatories by construing the word "incident" to cover all allegations of wrongdoing that they have alleged against the Academy. The word "incident" and the information called for in these interrogatories are sufficiently expansive to cover the allegations of wrongdoing in the complaint. Further responses are due no later than July 6, 2017. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Academy is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KAZUKO K. ARTUS VS. GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Application For An Order To Show Cause Regarding Why The Court Should Not Find Contempt And Order Sanctions For Violations Of The Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 6, 2018, Line 11, PLAINTIFF KAZUKO ARTUS' Application For An Order To Show Cause Regarding Why The Court Should Not Find Contempt And Order Sanctions For Violations Of The Preliminary Injunction. Plaintiff Kazuko Artus' application for an order to show cause why the court should not find contempt and order sanctions for violations of the preliminary injunction is denied. Dr. Artus has not shown that defendant Gramercy Towers Condominium Association willfully disobeyed the preliminary injunction order filed February 2, 2018. No election was held on January 30, 2018. GTCA appointed three inspectors for the February 6, 2018 election and all of them signed statements of independence and none of them have been shown to be biased in any way. While there is a plausible argument that the participation of Condominium Financial Management and persons affiliated with it in the February 6, 2018 was a technical violation of Civil Code 5110(b), there is no indication in the record that GTCA sought to avoid the requirements of the February 2 order by retaining CFM for the February 6 election. It is especially noteworthy that Dr. Artus does not contend that the there was any irregularity in the voting or counting of ballots for the February 6 election. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for GTCA is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ** TRANSFERRED TO SONOMA COUNTY ** CARL A. WESCOTT VS. MARLON YOUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 6, 2018, Line 14, DEFENDANTS MERRILL ARNONE & JONES LLP, MARLON YOUNG'S Motion To Change Venue. Defendants Marlon Young and Merrill, Arnone & Jones' motion to change venue is granted. The case is transferred to Sonoma County because that is where the defendants reside. (CCP 395). Plaintiff Carl Wescott is required to pay all required transfer fees. The fact that the Arnovick agreement required payment in San Francisco or that the defendants represented full payment to the bankruptcy trustee in San Francisco is irrelevant to the venue issue. Mr. Wescott and the defendants have no contractual relationship and Mr. Wescott has not sued the moving defendants for breach of contract. Mr. Wescott's tort claims are properly venued at defendants' residences in Sonoma County per CCP 395. Mr. Wescott's request that the action remain in San Francisco based on the interests of justice per CCP 397 is premature as there is no answer on file. (Forster v. Superior Court (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 782, 788.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
